### 31. [P2] Swallowed parent-sync exception silently leaves child Items unmapped, degrading bill/expense lines to NULL sub_cost_code

- **fid**: `ref-item-costcode-subcostcode#3` · **category**: error-swallow · **adversarial votes**: 2/3
- **file**: `integrations/intuit/qbo/item/business/service.py` · **lines**: 154-190

**What the code does:** _sync_to_cost_codes and _sync_to_sub_cost_codes wrap each connector call in `try/except Exception` that only logs and continues. If a parent item fails to sync (ItemCostCode mapping never created), every child of that parent then hits `if not parent_cost_code_mapping: raise ValueError('...not mapped...')` (sub_cost_code connector 73-79), also caught-and-logged. The child Item is left with no SubCostCode and no ItemSubCostCode mapping. Downstream, the bill line connector's missing-mapping fallback sets sub_cost_code_id = None (bill/connector/bill_line_item/business/service.py:110-116), so later bill/expense line pulls silently lose cost-code attribution.

**Root cause:** Broad per-item exception swallowing turns a single failing parent into a silent cascade across all its children, with no ReconciliationIssue/dead-letter row. The failure is invisible until a pulled bill line shows a NULL sub_cost_code.

**Failure trigger:** A transient DB error / row-version conflict / constraint failure on one parent CostCode during item sync, OR an incremental pull (last_updated_time) that returns a modified child whose parent is unmodified and was never full-synced.

**Edge cases:** Incremental sync returns child but not parent -> child permanently unmapped until a full re-sync; No reconciliation surfaces the gap

**Root-cause analysis (fix author):** The QBO Item pull (`QboItemService.sync_from_qbo`) splits fetched items into parents (no ParentRef → CostCode) and children (ParentRef → SubCostCode), then calls `_sync_to_cost_codes(parents)` followed by `_sync_to_sub_cost_codes(children)`. Both methods loop per item inside a blanket `try/except Exception` that does nothing but `logger.error(...)` and continue (service.py:167-171 and 186-190).

The defect is a silent two-stage cascade:

1. PARENT FAILS SILENTLY. `ItemCostCodeConnector.sync_from_qbo_item` is the only place an `ItemCostCode` mapping row is created. If that call raises for any reason (transient DB error, ROWVERSION/optimistic-concurrency conflict in `update_by_id`, a UNIQUE/FK constraint hit, duplicate-number race), the `except Exception` in `_sync_to_cost_codes` swallows it. No `ItemCostCode` mapping row is written for that parent QboItem. There is no ReconciliationIssue, no dead-letter, no re-raise — the run reports success.

2. EVERY CHILD OF THAT PARENT THEN FAILS SILENTLY. When `_sync_to_sub_cost_codes` reaches a child of the un-mapped parent, `ItemSubCostCodeConnector.sync_from_qbo_item` looks up the parent's CostCode mapping at sub_cost_code/service.py:73 and, finding none, raises `ValueError("Parent QboItem {id} is not mapped to a CostCode. Sync parent items first.")` (lines 75-79). That `ValueError` is again swallowed by the per-item `except Exception` in `_sync_to_sub_cost_codes`. The child QboItem row exists, but no SubCostCode is created and no `ItemSubCostCode` mapping is written.

3. DOWNSTREAM ATTRIBUTION IS LOST. Later, when a bill/expense line is pulled, `BillLineItemConnector.sync_from_qbo_bill_line` resolves the SubCostCode by walking `item_ref_value → QboItem → ItemSubCostCode mapping` (bill_line_item/service.py:104-118). With the `ItemSubCostCode` mapping missing, the lookup falls through to its else branch, leaves `sub_cost_code_id = None`, and the BillLineItem is persisted with NULL cost-code attribution — silently.

So a single failing parent (or, in incremental pulls, a parent that simply wasn't in this batch) silently strips cost-code attribution from all of its children and from every future bill/expense line that references those children, with zero operator-visible signal until someone notices a NULL sub_cost_code on a pulled line.

**Failure explanation:** In production this fires in two distinct, both-realistic ways:

A) TRANSIENT/CONFLICT FAILURE ON A PARENT DURING A FULL SYNC. The scheduler calls `POST /api/v1/admin/sync/qbo/item`, which runs `sync_from_qbo` with `sync_to_modules=True`. If one parent Item's CostCode upsert hits a transient SQL error, a ROWVERSION optimistic-concurrency conflict in `CostCodeService.repo.update_by_id`, or a duplicate-number race against another writer, the parent's `ItemCostCode` mapping is never created. Every child Item under that parent (often 5-30 sub cost codes per cost code) then raises "...not mapped..." and is skipped. The whole run logs at error level but returns a normal item list and exits 0. The scheduler sees success and won't retry. Nothing populates `[qbo].[ReconciliationIssue]`.

B) INCREMENTAL PULL RETURNS A CHILD BUT NOT ITS PARENT (the more insidious, finder-noted case). `sync_from_qbo(last_updated_time=...)` only fetches Items with `Metadata.LastUpdatedTime > watermark`. QBO timestamps parent and child independently. If a sub-item is edited in QBO but its parent cost-code item is untouched (and was never full-synced — e.g., a brand-new sub under an older parent, or a parent that predates this system), the incremental batch contains the child with an empty `parent_items` list. `_sync_to_sub_cost_codes` immediately raises "Parent QboItem ... not found" (sub_cost_code/service.py:69-70) or "...not mapped..." and swallows it. The child is PERMANENTLY unmapped until someone happens to run a full re-sync — and nothing tells anyone it needs one.

DATA IMPACT: BillLineItems and (by the same connector pattern) ExpenseLineItems pulled from QBO get `sub_cost_code_id = NULL`. That breaks cost-code reporting, the QBO push-back path (the memory notes "QBO sync requires every BillLineItem to have a sub_cost_code_id with a valid QBO Item mapping, otherwise lines are skipped" — so the line silently drops on the next push), Excel/SharePoint reconciliation that keys on cost code, and invoice packet enrichment (cost_code_number/name come from the SubCostCode join). The corruption is invisible at sync time and only surfaces as a missing cost code on a specific line days later.

**Edge-case analysis:** Edge cases the finder listed (both now covered): (1) transient/conflict failure on a parent during full sync → now records a CostCode issue and tracks the parent so children cite the cascade; (2) incremental pull returns child but not parent → child sync raises, now records a SubCostCode issue explicitly telling the operator a full re-sync is needed; (3) "no reconciliation surfaces the gap" → directly fixed.

Additional edge cases I found that the finder did NOT list:

a) ITEM ORDERING WITHIN A BATCH. `sync_from_qbo` collects all parents first, then all children, and `_sync_to_cost_codes` runs fully before `_sync_to_sub_cost_codes`. So within one batch a parent is always processed before its children — good. But this is only true because of the parent/child split, not because the QBO query is ordered. The fix does not rely on ordering; it relies on the `ItemCostCode` mapping existing (the connector reads it from the DB at line 73), which the parent pass establishes. No change needed, but worth noting the invariant.

b) GRANDCHILD / MULTI-LEVEL ITEMS. QBO supports nested sub-items (`Level` field exists on QboItem). The current code is strictly two-level (parent→CostCode, child→SubCostCode) and treats anything with a ParentRef as a child SubCostCode regardless of `Level`. A grandchild (sub-of-sub) whose immediate parent is itself a child would look up `cost_code_mapping_repo.read_by_qbo_item_id(parent)` and find none (the parent was mapped as a SubCostCode, not a CostCode), raising "...not mapped...". Previously silent; now this is surfaced as a SubCostCode missing_mapping issue — which is the correct behavior (it flags a real data-model mismatch the operator must decide on). The fix improves this case without trying to model multi-level items (out of scope).

c) DUPLICATE ISSUE ROWS ON REPEATED FAILURES. Every failed sync run writes a fresh ReconciliationIssue. A persistently-broken parent will accumulate one row per run (scheduler runs `sync/qbo/item` regularly). This is the same behavior the Bill reconciler has and is acceptable (issues carry a Status for dedupe/ack downstream), but operators should be aware the table can grow if a gap is left unresolved. If churn becomes a problem, an "open issue exists for this qbo_id+drift_type" guard could be added later — deliberately not added here to keep the fix minimal.

d) `qbo_id` MISSING ON A QboItem. `_record_unmapped_issue` passes `qbo_id=qbo_item.qbo_id`, which is Optional on the dataclass. The sproc allows NULL QboId, so this is safe; and `failed_parent_qbo_ids.add(item.qbo_id)` is guarded by `if item.qbo_id`. A parent with no qbo_id can't be matched as a child's parent anyway (children reference parents by qbo_id), so it correctly falls into the "not in batch" message branch.

e) CHILD WHOSE PARENT SYNCED FINE BUT THE CHILD ITSELF FAILS (e.g., transient DB error on the SubCostCode upsert, or a duplicate-number race at sub_cost_code/service.py:107-115). This child's parent is NOT in `failed_parent_qbo_ids`, so the message says "parent not in this batch" which would be slightly inaccurate. Minor cosmetic imprecision in the details string only — the issue is still correctly recorded and the line still gets flagged. Acceptable; the alternative (re-querying the ItemCostCode mapping to distinguish) adds a DB round-trip per failure for a wording nicety.

f) `realm_id` NULL on a QboItem. Coalesced to "" in the recorder. RealmId is NOT NULL-friendly conceptually but the sproc/column tolerance governs; passing "" avoids a None-typed bind issue. In practice every pulled QboItem carries realm_id (set in `_upsert_item`).

**Fix summary:** Two changes, both in `integrations/intuit/qbo/item/business/service.py`, minimal and aligned with the existing ReconciliationIssue infrastructure that the QBO Bill path already uses. No connector rewrite, no sproc change — the `[qbo].[ReconciliationIssue]` table, `CreateQboReconciliationIssue` sproc, `DRIFT_MISSING_MAPPING` drift type, and `_record_issue` machinery already exist and are entity-agnostic (`EntityType` is free text, parents → "CostCode", children → "SubCostCode").

1. Make parent-sync failures VISIBLE and TRACKED. In `_sync_to_cost_codes`, when a parent CostCode sync raises, still log+continue (don't fail the whole run — surviving children of healthy parents must still sync), but additionally (a) collect the failing parent's `qbo_id` into a `failed_parent_qbo_ids` set returned to the caller, and (b) write a `DRIFT_MISSING_MAPPING` ReconciliationIssue keyed on that parent so an operator/daily-reconcile sees it. This converts a silent log line into a durable, surfaced gap.

2. Make child-sync gaps VISIBLE in `_sync_to_sub_cost_codes`. Pass the `failed_parent_qbo_ids` set in. For any child whose sync raises (whether because its parent failed this run, its parent was never full-synced — the incremental case — or any other reason), write a `DRIFT_MISSING_MAPPING` ReconciliationIssue describing that the child Item is left unmapped and that a full parent re-sync is required. This is exactly the signal that was missing: the gap that previously only manifested as a downstream NULL sub_cost_code is now recorded at the moment it happens.

Issue recording is best-effort (wrapped so a recording failure can't crash the sync), mirroring `ReconciliationService._record_issue`. Per-item exception isolation is preserved — one bad item never aborts the batch — but the swallow is no longer silent. `realm_id` and `qbo_id` are read directly off each `QboItem` (the dataclass already carries both), so no method-signature plumbing of `realm_id` is needed.

This is the smallest change that closes the "invisible until a NULL appears" gap while keeping the resilient continue-on-error batch behavior the code intentionally has.

**Risk:** Low regression risk.

- Behavior of the happy path (parent+child both sync) is byte-for-byte unchanged — the new code only runs inside the `except` branches.
- Per-item exception isolation is preserved: one failing item still does not abort the batch, and a failure to RECORD an issue is itself swallowed (best-effort), so the recorder can never make things worse than before.
- `_sync_to_cost_codes` now returns a `set` instead of `None`. The only caller is `sync_from_qbo` (updated in the patch). Grep confirms these two private methods have no other callers. If any test asserts a `None` return from `_sync_to_cost_codes`, it would need updating — check test suite.
- New DB writes: one extra INSERT into `[qbo].[ReconciliationIssue]` per FAILED item only. On a healthy sync this is zero extra writes. On a broken sync it is bounded by the failure count. No new load on the hot path.
- Lazy import of the reconciliation module inside the recorder avoids any import-cycle risk at module load (matches existing ReconciliationService convention).

WATCH AFTER DEPLOY: (1) the `[qbo].[ReconciliationIssue]` table for a sudden influx of `missing_mapping` rows on first run — that is the fix working (surfacing a pre-existing silent backlog), not a new failure; triage them by running a full Item sync. (2) Confirm the scheduler's `sync/qbo/item` still returns success (the fix does not change the run's exit behavior — failures are still logged+continued, just now also recorded). (3) Watch logs for "Failed to record missing-mapping reconciliation issue" which would indicate the recorder itself is erroring (e.g., sproc/permission problem) — non-fatal but means the surfacing isn't landing.

**Test:** 1) UNIT — parent failure surfaces and cascades are attributed. Construct `QboItemService` with a mock `ItemCostCodeConnector` whose `sync_from_qbo_item` raises for one parent (e.g., a simulated `RowVersionConflict`/generic Exception), and a mock `ItemSubCostCodeConnector` that raises the real "...not mapped..." `ValueError` for that parent's child. Mock `ReconciliationIssueRepository.create`. Assert: (a) `_sync_to_cost_codes` returns a set containing the failed parent's qbo_id; (b) `create` was called with `entity_type="CostCode"`, `drift_type="missing_mapping"` for the parent; (c) `create` was called with `entity_type="SubCostCode"` for the child and the details string contains "cascade"; (d) the run still returns the full synced_items list (no abort).

2) UNIT — incremental-pull case (child without parent). Call `_sync_to_sub_cost_codes([child], failed_parent_qbo_ids=set())` where the connector raises "Parent QboItem ... not found". Assert a SubCostCode missing_mapping issue is recorded and its details contain "incremental pull" / "run a full Item sync".

3) UNIT — recorder is best-effort. Make `ReconciliationIssueRepository.create` raise; assert `_sync_to_cost_codes` / `_sync_to_sub_cost_codes` still complete without propagating and still process remaining items.

4) UNIT — happy path unchanged. Both connectors succeed → assert `create` is never called and return values are unchanged.

5) INTEGRATION (staging realm) — reproduce the real cascade end-to-end. Manually break a parent CostCode mapping (e.g., temporarily delete one `[qbo].[ItemCostCode]` row, or point a parent's number at a duplicate to force the upsert path), run `POST /api/v1/admin/sync/qbo/item`, then: (a) `SELECT * FROM [qbo].[ReconciliationIssue] WHERE DriftType='missing_mapping' ORDER BY CreatedDatetime DESC` — expect one CostCode row + N SubCostCode rows for the affected children; (b) pull a bill that references one of those children's Items and confirm the BillLineItem comes back with NULL sub_cost_code_id AND that the gap is now explained by an existing reconciliation issue (the whole point — the NULL is no longer unexplained). Then run a FULL Item sync (no last_updated_time) and confirm the children remap and no new missing_mapping rows appear.

6) REGRESSION — grep the test suite for any assertion on `_sync_to_cost_codes` returning None and update to expect a set.

**Patch:**

```diff
File: /Users/chris/Applications/build.one/build.one.api/integrations/intuit/qbo/item/business/service.py

--- BEFORE (lines 75-190) ---

        # Sync to modules if requested
        if sync_to_modules:
            self._sync_to_cost_codes(parent_items)
            self._sync_to_sub_cost_codes(child_items)
        
        return synced_items

    def _upsert_item(self, qbo_item: QboItemExternalSchema, realm_id: str) -> QboItem:
        ... (unchanged) ...

    def _sync_to_cost_codes(self, parent_items: List[QboItem]) -> None:
        """
        Sync parent items to CostCode module.
        
        Args:
            parent_items: List of parent QboItem records (no ParentRef)
        """
        if not parent_items:
            return
        
        connector = ItemCostCodeConnector()
        
        for item in parent_items:
            try:
                cost_code = connector.sync_from_qbo_item(item)
                logger.info(f"Synced QboItem {item.id} to CostCode {cost_code.id}")
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"Failed to sync QboItem {item.id} to CostCode: {e}")

    def _sync_to_sub_cost_codes(self, child_items: List[QboItem]) -> None:
        """
        Sync child items to SubCostCode module.
        
        Args:
            child_items: List of child QboItem records (with ParentRef)
        """
        if not child_items:
            return
        
        connector = ItemSubCostCodeConnector()
        
        for item in child_items:
            try:
                sub_cost_code = connector.sync_from_qbo_item(item)
                logger.info(f"Synced QboItem {item.id} to SubCostCode {sub_cost_code.id}")
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"Failed to sync QboItem {item.id} to SubCostCode: {e}")


--- AFTER ---

        # Sync to modules if requested
        if sync_to_modules:
            # Track parents that failed to map this run so child sync can
            # explain *why* a child was left unmapped (parent cascade) rather
            # than silently dropping it.
            failed_parent_qbo_ids = self._sync_to_cost_codes(parent_items)
            self._sync_to_sub_cost_codes(child_items, failed_parent_qbo_ids)
        
        return synced_items

    def _upsert_item(self, qbo_item: QboItemExternalSchema, realm_id: str) -> QboItem:
        ... (unchanged) ...

    def _record_unmapped_issue(
        self,
        *,
        entity_type: str,
        qbo_item: QboItem,
        details: str,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Best-effort: record a `missing_mapping` ReconciliationIssue so an
        unmapped Item is operator-visible instead of silently dropped.

        Recording is best-effort — a failure here must never abort the sync
        run (mirrors ReconciliationService._record_issue).
        """
        try:
            # Lazy import to avoid pulling the reconciliation stack at module load.
            from integrations.intuit.qbo.reconciliation.business.service import (
                DRIFT_MISSING_MAPPING,
            )
            from integrations.intuit.qbo.reconciliation.persistence.repo import (
                ReconciliationIssueRepository,
            )

            ReconciliationIssueRepository().create(
                drift_type=DRIFT_MISSING_MAPPING,
                severity="medium",
                action="flagged",
                entity_type=entity_type,
                realm_id=qbo_item.realm_id or "",
                qbo_id=qbo_item.qbo_id,
                details=details,
            )
        except Exception:
            logger.exception(
                "Failed to record missing-mapping reconciliation issue for "
                f"QboItem id={getattr(qbo_item, 'id', '?')} "
                f"qbo_id={getattr(qbo_item, 'qbo_id', '?')}"
            )

    def _sync_to_cost_codes(self, parent_items: List[QboItem]) -> set:
        """
        Sync parent items to CostCode module.

        Args:
            parent_items: List of parent QboItem records (no ParentRef)

        Returns:
            set[str]: QBO IDs of parent items that FAILED to sync/map this run.
                Used by _sync_to_sub_cost_codes to attribute child cascade
                failures to the upstream parent.
        """
        failed_parent_qbo_ids: set = set()
        if not parent_items:
            return failed_parent_qbo_ids

        connector = ItemCostCodeConnector()

        for item in parent_items:
            try:
                cost_code = connector.sync_from_qbo_item(item)
                logger.info(f"Synced QboItem {item.id} to CostCode {cost_code.id}")
            except Exception as e:
                # Per-item isolation: keep going so healthy parents still sync.
                # But this is NO LONGER silent — a failed parent un-maps every
                # one of its children, so surface it as a reconciliation issue.
                logger.error(f"Failed to sync QboItem {item.id} to CostCode: {e}")
                if item.qbo_id:
                    failed_parent_qbo_ids.add(item.qbo_id)
                self._record_unmapped_issue(
                    entity_type="CostCode",
                    qbo_item=item,
                    details=(
                        f"Parent QboItem id={item.id} qbo_id={item.qbo_id} "
                        f"('{item.name}') failed to sync to CostCode: "
                        f"{type(e).__name__}: {e}. Its child SubCostCodes will be "
                        f"left unmapped this run; re-run a full Item sync to recover."
                    ),
                )

        return failed_parent_qbo_ids

    def _sync_to_sub_cost_codes(
        self,
        child_items: List[QboItem],
        failed_parent_qbo_ids: Optional[set] = None,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Sync child items to SubCostCode module.

        Args:
            child_items: List of child QboItem records (with ParentRef)
            failed_parent_qbo_ids: QBO IDs of parents that failed to map this
                run (so a child cascade failure can name its cause).
        """
        if not child_items:
            return

        failed_parent_qbo_ids = failed_parent_qbo_ids or set()
        connector = ItemSubCostCodeConnector()

        for item in child_items:
            try:
                sub_cost_code = connector.sync_from_qbo_item(item)
                logger.info(f"Synced QboItem {item.id} to SubCostCode {sub_cost_code.id}")
            except Exception as e:
                # Per-item isolation preserved — but the gap is now recorded.
                # A swallowed failure here is exactly what silently degrades
                # downstream bill/expense lines to NULL sub_cost_code.
                logger.error(f"Failed to sync QboItem {item.id} to SubCostCode: {e}")

                parent_failed_this_run = (
                    item.parent_ref_value in failed_parent_qbo_ids
                )
                if parent_failed_this_run:
                    cause = (
                        f"its parent (qbo_id={item.parent_ref_value}) failed to "
                        f"sync to CostCode this run (cascade)"
                    )
                else:
                    # Most common in incremental pulls: child changed but parent
                    # was not in this batch and was never full-synced.
                    cause = (
                        f"its parent (qbo_id={item.parent_ref_value}) is not mapped "
                        f"to a CostCode (parent not in this sync batch — likely an "
                        f"incremental pull; run a full Item sync to map the parent)"
                    )
                self._record_unmapped_issue(
                    entity_type="SubCostCode",
                    qbo_item=item,
                    details=(
                        f"Child QboItem id={item.id} qbo_id={item.qbo_id} "
                        f"('{item.name}') left UNMAPPED to a SubCostCode because "
                        f"{cause}. Error: {type(e).__name__}: {e}. Until remapped, "
                        f"bill/expense lines referencing this Item will pull with "
                        f"sub_cost_code_id = NULL."
                    ),
                )


NOTE: `Optional` is already imported (line 3: `from typing import List, Optional`), and `set` is builtin — no new top-level imports are required. The reconciliation imports are lazy (inside `_record_unmapped_issue`) to match the lazy-import discipline used by ReconciliationService and avoid any import cycle at module load.
```


---

### 32. [P2] Project.Description unconditionally overwritten (and blanked to '') from QBO Customer.Notes on every pull

- **fid**: `ref-vendor-customer-project#3` · **category**: null-overwrite · **adversarial votes**: 3/3
- **file**: `integrations/intuit/qbo/customer/connector/project/business/service.py` · **lines**: 73-101

**What the code does:** On the update branch it sets project.description = project_description where project_description = qbo_customer.notes or '' (line 74), then calls project_service.repo.update_by_id(project). UpdateProjectById (entities/project/sql/dbo.project.sql:277-285) SETs [Description] = @Description unconditionally — no CASE WHEN guard (only CustomerId is guarded).

**Root cause:** QBO Customer.Notes is mapped onto the internal Project.Description and pushed through an unconditional UPDATE. There is no field-ownership entry for Project in base/field_ownership.py, so Description's ownership is undefined; the connector treats it as QBO-owned and clobbers it. When qbo_customer.notes is None the `or ''` coerces it to empty string, so a pull positively erases any human-entered Project.Description rather than leaving it untouched. Project.Notes and Abbreviation survive only because the connector re-reads them via read_by_id and re-passes them; Description is the one field it overwrites.

**Failure trigger:** Any customer pull touching a job-customer whose local Project has a human-maintained Description while QBO Customer.Notes is empty/different — Description wiped/overwritten each sync.

**Edge cases:** Empty-string overwrite is worse than NULL — bypasses any 'preserve when NULL' guard had one existed; Project absent from field_ownership.py, so reviewers have no contract flagging Description as app-owned

**Root-cause analysis (fix author):** The QBO Customer→Project pull connector (`CustomerProjectConnector.sync_from_qbo_customer`) maps QBO `Customer.Notes` onto the internal `Project.Description` field and then pushes that mapping through an unconditional UPDATE.

Mechanism, step by step:
1. Line 74 computes `project_description = qbo_customer.notes or ""`. The `or ""` means that whenever `qbo_customer.notes` is None or empty, `project_description` becomes the empty string `""` (not None).
2. On the update branch (mapping exists, lines 92-101), the connector re-reads the existing local Project via `self.project_service.read_by_id(...)` (so `name`, `description`, `status`, `customer_id`, `abbreviation`, `notes` all reflect current DB state), then OVERWRITES `project.description = project_description` (line 98) before calling `self.project_service.repo.update_by_id(project)` (line 101).
3. `ProjectRepository.update_by_id` (repo.py:264-289) passes EVERY column to `UpdateProjectById`, including `Description = project.description`.
4. `UpdateProjectById` (dbo.project.sql:277-285) executes `SET ... [Description] = @Description` UNCONDITIONALLY. Unlike `[CustomerId]`, which has a `CASE WHEN @CustomerId IS NULL THEN [CustomerId] ELSE @CustomerId END` preserve-on-NULL guard, `[Description]` has no guard at all.

The reason `Notes` and `Abbreviation` survive a pull while `Description` does not is that the connector never reassigns `project.notes` or `project.abbreviation` — it leaves the re-read values in place, so `update_by_id` writes back the same DB value (a harmless no-op). `Description` is the single field the connector actively clobbers with QBO-derived data.

Why this is wrong by the codebase's own contract: `integrations/intuit/qbo/base/field_ownership.py` declares which side owns each field for bidirectional sync. Pull paths "MUST NOT overwrite app-owned fields." But there is NO `Project`/`Customer→Project` entry in that registry, and `ownership_of()` defaults unknown fields to `QBO_OWNED`. So the connector implicitly treats `Description` as QBO-owned and overwrites it on every pull. In reality `Project.Description` is a human-maintained local field (set via the React Project edit page / `ProjectService.update_by_public_id`), and QBO `Customer.Notes` is not its source of truth — it is a semantically different field that was incorrectly mapped.

**Failure explanation:** Production failure trigger: any QBO Customer pull that touches a job/sub-customer (`Job=true`) whose local Project already exists (mapping present → update branch) and whose `Project.Description` has been edited by a human, while the corresponding QBO `Customer.Notes` is empty or holds different text.

Customer pulls run on a schedule (`POST /api/v1/admin/sync/qbo/customer` driven by the scheduler) and also via the reconciler. On each such pull:
- If QBO `Customer.Notes` is NULL/empty (the common case — almost nobody fills in the Notes field on a QBO sub-customer): `project_description` resolves to `""`, and the human-entered `Project.Description` is ERASED, replaced with an empty string.
- If QBO `Customer.Notes` holds unrelated text: the human-entered `Project.Description` is OVERWRITTEN with that unrelated text.

Data impact: silent, recurring data loss on a customer-facing/operational field. Every sync cycle re-applies the clobber, so even if a human re-enters the Description it is wiped again on the next pull — there is no stable state. Because the write goes through the normal Project UPDATE sproc (and Project changes are audited via the workflow/`ModifiedDatetime`), the only forensic trail is the modified timestamp; the prior Description content is gone. This is strictly worse than a NULL overwrite because `""` (empty string) would defeat any future "preserve when NULL" guard added to the sproc — a NULL-only guard would not catch the empty-string write.

**Edge-case analysis:** 1. Empty-string vs NULL (finder noted): `qbo_customer.notes or ""` coerces None to `""`. A sproc-level "preserve when NULL" guard would NOT protect Description because the connector never sends NULL — it sends `""`. The fix must live in the connector (stop sending the value) OR the sproc guard must treat empty string as "preserve" too. Connector fix is cleaner and avoids changing global UPDATE semantics for the React edit page (where clearing the field SHOULD be allowed to set it blank).

2. CREATE branch also maps Notes→Description (lines 74, 147-150, and the name-match-bind branch implicitly creates via `create`). On first import a brand-new Project gets `description = qbo_customer.notes or ""`. This is less harmful (no human value to destroy yet) but it (a) silently stuffs QBO Notes into a field humans think they own, and (b) sets Description to `""` instead of NULL on the vast majority of customers whose Notes are empty, creating empty-string noise rows. After the fix, new Projects should be created with Description = NULL (no QBO mapping) so humans start from a clean slate.

3. Name-match BIND branch (lines 118-141): when an existing unmapped local Project is found by name and bound, the connector returns `existing_local` WITHOUT touching its Description — so that path is already safe and must stay that way. The fix should not regress it.

4. RowVersion optimistic-concurrency interaction: `update_by_id` includes `RowVersion` in the WHERE clause. If the connector writes nothing meaningful (after fix it may still need to update name/status/customer_id), it still bumps RowVersion. That's acceptable and pre-existing; not introduced by the fix.

5. Edge not listed by finder — the connector re-reads via `project_service.read_by_id`, which is UserProject-scoped. Outbox/sync workers set `is_system_admin=True` so the read returns the row; if a future caller of this connector lacks that context, `read_by_id` could return None and the connector would mis-route to the "recreate" branch (line 107-111) — separate latent issue, out of scope, but worth flagging: the Description clobber is not the only fragility on this update branch.

6. Edge not listed by finder — field_ownership.py has NO Project/Customer entry, and `ownership_of()` DEFAULTS unknown entities/fields to QBO_OWNED. So `Description`, AND every other Project field, is undeclared. Adding a `CUSTOMER_PROJECT` ownership entry documents that Description/Notes/Abbreviation are APP_OWNED and only Name/Status (and Customer linkage) are QBO-derived — protecting future connector authors from re-introducing the same bug on `notes`/`abbreviation`.

7. Edge not listed by finder — `Status` is also being forced from QBO (`"active"/"inactive"` from `qbo_customer.active`). That IS legitimately QBO-derived (matches the QBO active flag), so leaving it overwritten is correct; but note that the filtered unique index `UQ_Project_Name_CustomerId_Active` keys on `Status='active'`, so a QBO-driven status flip can change uniqueness eligibility. Not part of this bug; just adjacent.

**Fix summary:** Minimal, robust fix — stop the connector from mapping QBO `Customer.Notes` onto the app-owned `Project.Description`:

1. UPDATE branch (primary fix): delete the `project.description = project_description` assignment (line 98). The connector already re-reads the full Project via `read_by_id`, so leaving `project.description` untouched means `update_by_id` writes back the existing DB value (the same harmless no-op that already protects `notes` and `abbreviation`). Human-entered Description is preserved across every pull. Name, Status, and CustomerId continue to be QBO-driven as before.

2. CREATE path: stop seeding Description from QBO Notes. Create the Project with `description=None` (the table allows NULL) so new Projects start blank and humans own the field from the start. Remove the now-unused `project_description` computation (line 74) to avoid a misleading dead variable, and drop the `description=...` kwarg from the `create()` call. (`ProjectService.create`'s `description` param has no default, so pass `description=None` explicitly rather than omitting it.)

3. Documentation/contract (defense in depth): add a `CUSTOMER_PROJECT` entry to `integrations/intuit/qbo/base/field_ownership.py` declaring `description`, `notes`, `abbreviation` as `app_owned` and `name`, `status`, `customer_id` as `qbo_owned`, and register it under `"Project"` / `"Customer→Project"`. This gives reviewers a machine-readable contract so the bug can't silently reappear when someone edits this connector.

Why connector-level (not sproc-level): the unconditional `SET [Description] = @Description` in `UpdateProjectById` is CORRECT for the React edit page, where a user clearing the textarea must be able to blank the field. Guarding the sproc on NULL would (a) not even fix this bug — the connector sends `""`, not NULL — and (b) break legitimate human blanking. The right layer is the connector, which should simply never own/overwrite Description. No SQL change is required.

**Risk:** Low regression risk.

What changes behaviorally:
- A QBO customer pull no longer modifies Project.Description on existing Projects. This is the intended fix; the only "loss" is the (incorrect) propagation of QBO Customer.Notes into Description, which nobody should depend on.
- New Projects created by the connector now get Description = NULL instead of "" or QBO Notes text. Downstream code that reads Project.Description must tolerate NULL — the column is already declared NULLABLE (dbo.project.sql:16), the React edit page handles NULL (it sends "" only on explicit clear), and ProjectService.update_by_public_id already round-trips NULL. Low risk.

What to watch:
- Confirm nothing reads Project.Description expecting QBO Customer.Notes content (grep for `.description` on Project consumers, FindProjectForInvoice does NOT return Description, so the agents are unaffected — they read Project.Notes).
- The connector still bumps RowVersion / ModifiedDatetime on each pull because Name/Status/CustomerId are still written unconditionally. That is pre-existing behavior, not introduced here.
- field_ownership.py is currently documentation/reference only (not enforced at runtime per its own docstring), so the registry addition cannot itself break anything; it only guides reviewers.
- One-time cleanup: existing Projects whose Description was already wiped to "" or overwritten with QBO Notes are NOT auto-restored by this fix. Prior values are unrecoverable from the app; if needed, restore from a DB backup / point-in-time. After deploy, the field stops being clobbered, so re-entered Descriptions will finally persist.

**Test:** 1. Unit/connector test (primary, proves the fix):
   - Arrange: a local Project with Description="Hand-written job scope notes", bound via CustomerProject mapping to a QboCustomer. Build a QboCustomer with is_job=True, notes=None (and a second case notes="some unrelated QBO note"), active=True.
   - Act: call `CustomerProjectConnector.sync_from_qbo_customer(qbo_customer)` (stub the repos: mapping_repo.read_by_qbo_customer_id returns the mapping; project_service.read_by_id returns the Project; capture the Project passed to project_service.repo.update_by_id).
   - Assert: the Project handed to update_by_id has description == "Hand-written job scope notes" UNCHANGED in BOTH cases (notes=None and notes="unrelated"). Also assert name/status/customer_id were updated from QBO. This is the exact regression that fails on the current code (it would be "" / "unrelated") and passes after the fix.

2. Create-path test:
   - Arrange: no mapping, no name-match local Project, QboCustomer with notes="x".
   - Act: sync_from_qbo_customer.
   - Assert: project_service.create was called with description=None (NOT "x", NOT "").

3. Name-match-bind regression guard:
   - Arrange: an existing unmapped local Project found by name with Description="keep me".
   - Act: sync.
   - Assert: update_by_id / create were NOT called with a mutated description; the existing Project is returned untouched.

4. Integration smoke (prod-shaped): pick a known job-customer Project, set Description via the React edit page, confirm the corresponding QBO Customer.Notes is empty, then run `POST /api/v1/admin/sync/qbo/customer` (with DRAIN_SECRET) and re-read the Project — Description must be intact. Run the sync twice to confirm idempotent stability (the original bug re-wiped on every cycle).

5. field_ownership assertion (cheap): `for_entity("Project").ownership_of("description") == APP_OWNED` and `ownership_of("name") == QBO_OWNED`.

**Patch:**

```diff
FILE 1: /Users/chris/Applications/build.one/build.one.api/integrations/intuit/qbo/customer/connector/project/business/service.py

--- update branch + field mapping ---

BEFORE (lines 72-101):
        # Map QBO Customer fields to Project module fields
        project_name = qbo_customer.display_name or qbo_customer.company_name or ""
        project_description = qbo_customer.notes or ""
        project_status = "active" if qbo_customer.active else "inactive"

        # Find the parent Customer ID if this job has a parent
        customer_id = None
        if qbo_customer.parent_ref_value:
            # Look up the parent QboCustomer and its mapping to Customer
            qbo_customer_repo = QboCustomerRepository()
            parent_qbo_customer = qbo_customer_repo.read_by_qbo_id(qbo_customer.parent_ref_value)
            if parent_qbo_customer:
                parent_mapping = self.customer_mapping_repo.read_by_qbo_customer_id(parent_qbo_customer.id)
                if parent_mapping:
                    customer_id = parent_mapping.customer_id
                    logger.debug(f"Found parent Customer {customer_id} for Project")

        # Check for existing mapping
        mapping = self.mapping_repo.read_by_qbo_customer_id(qbo_customer.id)

        if mapping:
            # Found existing mapping - update the Project
            project = self.project_service.read_by_id(mapping.project_id)
            if project:
                logger.info(f"Updating existing Project {project.id} from QboCustomer {qbo_customer.id}")
                project.name = project_name
                project.description = project_description
                project.status = project_status
                project.customer_id = customer_id
                project = self.project_service.repo.update_by_id(project)

AFTER:
        # Map QBO Customer fields to Project module fields.
        # NOTE: Project.Description is APP-OWNED (human-maintained via the
        # React Project edit page) — it is NOT QBO Customer.Notes and must
        # NEVER be overwritten on a pull. Mapping Notes->Description here
        # erased human-entered Descriptions on every sync (empty Notes ->
        # "" clobber). See base/field_ownership.py CUSTOMER_PROJECT.
        project_name = qbo_customer.display_name or qbo_customer.company_name or ""
        project_status = "active" if qbo_customer.active else "inactive"

        # Find the parent Customer ID if this job has a parent
        customer_id = None
        if qbo_customer.parent_ref_value:
            # Look up the parent QboCustomer and its mapping to Customer
            qbo_customer_repo = QboCustomerRepository()
            parent_qbo_customer = qbo_customer_repo.read_by_qbo_id(qbo_customer.parent_ref_value)
            if parent_qbo_customer:
                parent_mapping = self.customer_mapping_repo.read_by_qbo_customer_id(parent_qbo_customer.id)
                if parent_mapping:
                    customer_id = parent_mapping.customer_id
                    logger.debug(f"Found parent Customer {customer_id} for Project")

        # Check for existing mapping
        mapping = self.mapping_repo.read_by_qbo_customer_id(qbo_customer.id)

        if mapping:
            # Found existing mapping - update the Project
            project = self.project_service.read_by_id(mapping.project_id)
            if project:
                logger.info(f"Updating existing Project {project.id} from QboCustomer {qbo_customer.id}")
                project.name = project_name
                # Description is app-owned: leave the re-read value in place.
                project.status = project_status
                project.customer_id = customer_id
                project = self.project_service.repo.update_by_id(project)


--- create path (no existing local Project) ---

BEFORE (lines 143-150):
        # No existing local Project — create one and pair it with a mapping.
        logger.info(f"Creating new Project from QboCustomer {qbo_customer.id}: name={project_name}")
        project = self.project_service.create(
            name=project_name,
            description=project_description,
            status=project_status,
            customer_id=customer_id
        )

AFTER:
        # No existing local Project — create one and pair it with a mapping.
        # Description is app-owned — start it blank (NULL); never seed it
        # from QBO Customer.Notes.
        logger.info(f"Creating new Project from QboCustomer {qbo_customer.id}: name={project_name}")
        project = self.project_service.create(
            name=project_name,
            description=None,
            status=project_status,
            customer_id=customer_id
        )


FILE 2: /Users/chris/Applications/build.one/build.one.api/integrations/intuit/qbo/base/field_ownership.py

Add a new ownership block after the VENDOR_CREDIT definition (after line 184), and register it in _REGISTRY.

BEFORE (lines 186-197):
# Lookup by entity name (both local name and QBO name for convenience).
_REGISTRY: Dict[str, FieldOwnership] = {
    # Local entity name → ownership rules.
    "Bill": BILL,
    "Invoice": INVOICE,
    "Expense": PURCHASE,
    "BillCredit": VENDOR_CREDIT,
    # QBO entity name aliases.
    "Purchase": PURCHASE,
    "VendorCredit": VENDOR_CREDIT,
}

AFTER:
# Project ⟵ QBO Customer (Job=true sub-customers map to Project).
# Description / Notes / Abbreviation are human-maintained in the React
# Project edit page; the customer pull MUST NOT touch them. Only Name,
# Status (from Customer.Active) and the parent Customer linkage are
# QBO-derived. (Was the source of a silent Description-wipe bug: the
# connector mapped QBO Customer.Notes -> Project.Description and pushed
# it through an unconditional UPDATE.)
CUSTOMER_PROJECT = FieldOwnership(
    entity="Project",   # local entity name; corresponds to QBO Customer (Job=true)
    qbo_owned=[
        "name",          # DisplayName / CompanyName
        "status",        # derived from Customer.Active
        "customer_id",   # parent Customer linkage (ParentRef)
    ],
    app_owned=[
        "description",   # human-maintained; NOT QBO Customer.Notes
        "notes",         # free-text per-project guidance for the agents
        "abbreviation",  # human-maintained
    ],
    both_editable=[],
)


# Lookup by entity name (both local name and QBO name for convenience).
_REGISTRY: Dict[str, FieldOwnership] = {
    # Local entity name → ownership rules.
    "Bill": BILL,
    "Invoice": INVOICE,
    "Expense": PURCHASE,
    "BillCredit": VENDOR_CREDIT,
    "Project": CUSTOMER_PROJECT,
    # QBO entity name aliases.
    "Purchase": PURCHASE,
    "VendorCredit": VENDOR_CREDIT,
    "Customer→Project": CUSTOMER_PROJECT,
}

No SQL change required — UpdateProjectById's unconditional SET [Description] is correct for the React edit page (clearing the textarea must blank the field); the fix is to stop the connector from owning Description.
```
